title: [v5.4-rhel] support new crun error messages
body:

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v5.4-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Do we want to rebase/cherry-pick a newer crun into RHEL? I thought RHEL is completely frozen now.

Shouldn't we run podman@main/v5.5. for rhel 10.1 tests?

cc @TomSweeneyRedHat
Best we can do with this is 10.0.1 Zstream.  Regardless, we need a Jira card for this to backport, is there one @giuseppe ?
Adding a hold on this until the ZeroDay goes out in a couple of weeks.

@giuseppe The 10.0 ZeroDay is frozen at this point so that our QE team has a chance to get through testing the 20 or so cards they already have for it by the 14th of this month.  Is this an issue that will completely break Podman without it in place?  I've also asked Jindrich if this is stopping the required tests for packaging for RHEL 9.6/10.0.  If it hits one of those criteria, we can move it forward.  If not, we'll have to wait for the next Zstream release.
it breaks podman with the new crun version.  We either block crun updates (and that can be a problem for OCP) or we backport this patch.

My understanding is that the crun update is tested in the 10.1 branch against the podman 5.4 build. Instead the crun update should be tested against podman@main or 5.5 once released, as such I don't quite see the need to backport this change. I though the crun we ship in RHEL 10.0 will not be 1.21 so it wouldn't need the error message fixes?
But maybe I am missing something here.

cc @jnovy

@Luap99 Your understanding is correct, the RHEL10.1 is tested against v5.4-rhel branch until 5.5 is released. At the moment RHEL10.0 0day contains the same content meaning we need this backport for RHEL10.0/9.6 0day in order to make gating tests pass as we would need to fix it in 10.0/9.6 anyway in the future. I don't see any reason why not to backport this fix.

LGTM
 and we have the Jira cards lines up for this
